mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in retail sales indicates that household demand is absorbing the pandemic shock and lifting expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS is rising on a 3-month basis.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 549256.0 as of two months before 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A downward crossing of the unemployment rate through 7.0% signals that labor-market slack is falling fast enough to support income growth and risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 7.0% after being above 7.0% in the prior monthly observation
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 6.9 as of one month before 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered crossings below 7.0% produce average 20-day S&P 500 returns less than or equal to the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero while CPI is still rising, discount-rate support dominates inflation concern and increases the present value of expected earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 0.09 as of one month before 2020-11-15
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 260.325 as of one month before 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and rising CPIAUCSL have non-positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum captures the speed of reopening in goods output; a positive 3-month change in INDPRO raises expected earnings for cyclicals and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 101.5055 as of two months before 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month INDPRO changes.

mechanism_narrative: A housing starts breakout above 1400 thousand units indicates strong interest-rate-sensitive demand and construction activity, raising expected cyclical earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 1400 thousand units and its 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 1415.0 as of two months before 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakouts above 1400 with positive 3-month momentum do not outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid CPI acceleration while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5% signals an inflation scare that erodes real purchasing power and raises uncertainty about future tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-scare episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent payroll gains and a falling unemployment rate confirm that labor demand is improving rather than reflecting labor-force distortions, reducing recession risk and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value was 142373.0 as of one month before 2020-11-15
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 6.9 as of one month before 2020-11-15
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll gains and falling unemployment fail to produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than payroll gains alone.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with flat or declining industrial production signals demand running ahead of supply, which pressures margins through bottlenecks and inventory shortages, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if demand-supply divergence episodes have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered observations.
